prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: When combobox widget requested, use custom listbox - native select alone is insufficient Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Reference DOM (Placeholder-only identification): Empty label element; field identified only by placeholder attribute Reference DOM (Conditional radio reveal): Selecting Yes on sponsorship reveals follow-up textarea Reference DOM (Fields inside details accordion): Cover letter and visa fields hidden inside closed details element Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: label-wraps-deep, shadow-host, native-select-not-combobox, duplicate-name-attrs, details-accordion. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: two-column grid without card wrapper. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Placeholder-only identification; Conditional radio reveal; Fields inside details accordion.
